barks.text: Empty rooms talk loudest, son. — G.G.
Befriend whoever pours the coffee. The rest follows. — G.G.
A landlady who quotes exactly is worth three constables.
Ha! Mine was plums. Every reporter has a nemesis fruit. Hereditary, apparently — check the jam.
Ida was old when I was young. The drawers really do get ideas.
The nervous ones are the honest ones. Remember that in about four days.
A man who rehearses joy is not your thief.
Watch the hands, not the smile. You did. Good.
This one interviews YOU. Steal the technique. Credit her in the footnotes of your conscience.
Every town issues one of these. I was mine.
Rooms remember. Told you that one myself, didn't I. Still true.
Dust is the honestest witness there is. Never bribed one yet.
See? Honest. And you were kind about it. That's the whole trade.
A sketch shows what it means. You quoted me. I'm insufferable about it.
Between us and the sherry. You kept it that way. Proud of you.
Forty-seven. I only ever saw eleven. Somebody should finish the count.
Photographs show the truth. Almost never the one you developed them for.
The loud suspect is the free witness. Works every county I tried it in.
Gentle hands from here, son. You know why now.
She handed you the family. Notice you never dropped it.
A secret for a secret. The grape thing was a fair price. Ask me how I know.
Half a conversation starts most of the world's trouble. The other half usually forgives it.
Sit on the floor with him. Good lad.
Lead together, argue often, decide once. A whole town in eleven words. I never managed a lede under twenty.
The jam keeps. So does the rule. Go find the next story. — G.G.
